summary: End-to-end testing of the Jan 2026 iteration: (1) DSG Music Group blueprint at /api/music-group/*, (2) Admin Logistics dashboard at /admin/dsg-logistics, (3) sandbox_firewall refactor. Backend regression_shield 593/593 PASS. New integration suite (15 tests) PASS. Service-level unit suite (4 tests) PASS — burn_coins=0 invariant verified, share-sum reconciliation verified (1000 and 999 both reconcile exactly). Frontend smoke: /artist/music-group renders [data-testid=music-group-panel], /admin/dsg-logistics renders [data-testid=admin-dsg-logistics-page], /explore Cmd+K registry contains 'DSG Music Group', /dsg-tv and /dsg-logistics regressions remain green. Sandbox firewall refactor verified: utils/sandbox_firewall.py exists with install(app,db,logger), server.py imports + calls it, inline async def _sandbox_firewall is REMOVED from server.py.

issues: When a non-admin user navigates here, the page chrome still renders and the data fetches return 403 in the console. The shell is gated only by ProtectedRoute (auth) — not by an admin role check on the client. This is functional (server enforces 403) but the page should ideally short-circuit with an access-denied state for non-admins to avoid confusing 403 console errors.

action_items: Optional polish: AdminDSGLogistics page should render an explicit 'admin required' panel for non-admin users instead of attempting the protected API calls and surfacing 403 in console (currently functional but noisy).

Optional: the regression_shield's D1 assertion currently checks for '_sandbox_firewall' as a substring inside utils/sandbox_firewall.py — that's fine, but a stricter assertion 'async def _sandbox_firewall' was tightened in test_dsg_music_group_iter.test_sandbox_firewall_extracted to guarantee the inline handler is not left over in server.py.

Optional: in dsg_music_group.set_collaborator_splits, ownership is validated BEFORE bps invariant. UX-wise the artist may want bps-validation feedback even when track_id is wrong — current order is correct security-wise, no change required.
critical_code_review_comments: services/dsg_music_group.py disburse_collective_royalty: rounding remainder is absorbed by the LAST entry in the bps-DESC-sorted list — that's the SMALLEST bps holder. Comment in code says 'largest holder absorbs rounding' which is opposite. Functionally fine (reconciliation still exact), but the comment/sort direction are inconsistent. Cosmetic only.
routes/dsg_music_group_routes.py imports BPS_TOTAL/MAX/ARTIST_COLLECTIVE_PCT directly — fine; constants endpoint is public and harmless.
utils/sandbox_firewall.py: best-effort security_events write swallows ALL exceptions silently (BLE001). Acceptable here because handler must not fail — documented in comment.
burn_coins=0 hardcoded in disburse — matches blueprint hard rule; ledger row confirmed via unit test.

success_rate.backend: 100% (593 regression + 15 new integration + 4 service unit = 612/612 PASS)

test_credentials: Beta tester betatester1@globalvibez.com / BetaTester2026! (auto-seeded, not admin role).
seed_data_creation: Unit suite creates+cleans test_mg_track_001 in dsg_tracks; cleanup runs after each test. No production data mutated.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Music Group + Admin Logistics + sandbox firewall refactor all GREEN. No admin user in the seeded credential set, so admin happy-path for /admin/music-group/royalty/disburse + /admin/dsg-logistics endpoints is covered only at the 401/403 guard level via HTTP and at the service level (which bypasses the admin guard) via the unit suite. If E1 adds an admin in /app/memory/test_credentials.md, re-run with admin login to exercise admin_disburse end-to-end. Also: AdminDSGLogistics shell renders for non-admins because client route only checks auth; consider adding a client-side admin-role gate (see design_issues).
rca of the issue: N/A — no defects found in this iteration.
